Source URL: https://www.sbdc.uh.edu/sbdc/Cybersecurity.asp

# SBDC Cybersecurity Resource Hub

## Resource type
Cybersecurity and resilience resource hub.

## Best for
Businesses needing cyber risk awareness, cyber planning, ransomware/phishing/mobile security information, FTC/NIST/FCC/CISA resources, cybersecurity webinars, and cybersecurity specialist routing.

## Best-fit BOSS lifecycle stages
Formation, Launch, Operations, Growth, Recovery.

## Use when the entrepreneur says
"I’m worried about cyber risk," "I need cyber insurance," "I got hacked," "I need a security plan," "I want CMMC/government contracting cybersecurity guidance."

## Resource summary
The page includes no-cost cybersecurity resources, a cybersecurity specialist, webinars, Data Assured program, Cyber Workbook, ransomware/mobile/phishing resources, FTC/NIST/FCC/CISA links, and other cyber guidance.

## What the user can do with this resource
Use resources, request SBDC specialist referral, create/review a basic cybersecurity action plan, and route urgent incidents to appropriate cyber/legal/professional help.

## BOSS routing notes
Do not provide incident-response, forensic, legal, or compliance guarantees. Route urgent breaches to qualified professionals and authorities where appropriate.

## Related source URLs
- https://www.sbdc.uh.edu/sbdc/Business_Specialists.asp

## BOSS Library use notes
- Treat this as a resource-routing document, not as legal, tax, lending, certification, or procurement advice.
- Use the linked source URL as the user-facing official link.
- Ask discovery questions before routing when the user is early-stage or unclear.
- For Houston/77084 users, verify county/service-area fit through the official SBDC/SBA locator before giving a final local-office referral.
